name: Sum of a Pair
statement:
Given t pairs of integers, output the sum of each pair.

Input:
The first line contains an integer t — the number of test cases.
Each of the next t lines contains two integers a and b.

Output:
For each test case, print a + b on its own line.

Constraints:
1 <= t <= 1000
-10^9 <= a, b <= 10^9

name: Maximum of Three
statement:
For each test case, print the largest of three integers.

Input:
The first line contains an integer t — the number of test cases.
Each of the next t lines contains three integers a b c.

Output:
For each test case, print max(a, b, c) on its own line.

Constraints:
1 <= t <= 1000
-10^9 <= a, b, c <= 10^9

name: Even or Odd
statement:
For each integer, print whether it is Even or Odd.

Input:
The first line contains an integer t — the number of test cases.
Each of the next t lines contains one integer n.

Output:
For each test case, print Even or Odd on its own line.

Constraints:
1 <= t <= 1000
-10^9 <= n <= 10^9

name: Factorial
statement:
For each n, print n! (the product 1*2*...*n). By definition 0! = 1.

Input:
The first line contains an integer t — the number of test cases.
Each of the next t lines contains one integer n.

Output:
For each test case, print n! on its own line.

Constraints:
1 <= t <= 1000
0 <= n <= 20

name: Reverse a String
statement:
For each word, print it reversed. Words contain no spaces.

Input:
The first line contains an integer t — the number of test cases.
Each of the next t lines contains one word s.

Output:
For each test case, print the reversed word on its own line.

Constraints:
1 <= t <= 1000
1 <= |s| <= 100

name: Count the Vowels
statement:
For each word, count its vowels (a e i o u, case-insensitive).

Input:
The first line contains an integer t — the number of test cases.
Each of the next t lines contains one word s.

Output:
For each test case, print the vowel count on its own line.

Constraints:
1 <= t <= 1000
1 <= |s| <= 100

name: N-th Fibonacci
statement:
Print the n-th Fibonacci number (0-indexed): F(0)=0, F(1)=1, F(k)=F(k-1)+F(k-2).

Input:
The first line contains an integer t — the number of test cases.
Each of the next t lines contains one integer n.

Output:
For each test case, print F(n) on its own line.

Constraints:
1 <= t <= 1000
0 <= n <= 90

name: Greatest Common Divisor
statement:
For each pair, print gcd(a, b). By convention gcd(0, x) = x.

Input:
The first line contains an integer t — the number of test cases.
Each of the next t lines contains two integers a b.

Output:
For each test case, print gcd(a, b) on its own line.

Constraints:
1 <= t <= 1000
0 <= a, b <= 10^9

name: Palindrome Check
statement:
For each word, print YES if it reads the same forwards and backwards, else NO.

Input:
The first line contains an integer t — the number of test cases.
Each of the next t lines contains one word s.

Output:
For each test case, print YES or NO on its own line.

Constraints:
1 <= t <= 1000
1 <= |s| <= 100

name: Sum 1 to N
statement:
For each n, print 1 + 2 + ... + n. For n = 0 the answer is 0.

Input:
The first line contains an integer t — the number of test cases.
Each of the next t lines contains one integer n.

Output:
For each test case, print the sum on its own line.

Constraints:
1 <= t <= 1000
0 <= n <= 10^6

name: Array Sum
statement:
For each array, print the sum of its elements.

Input:
The first line contains an integer t — the number of test cases.
For each test case, the first line contains n, and the next line contains n integers.

Output:
For each test case, print the sum on its own line.

Constraints:
1 <= t <= 100
1 <= n <= 10^5
-10^9 <= each element <= 10^9

name: Count Distinct Elements
statement:
For each array, print the number of distinct values.

Input:
The first line contains an integer t — the number of test cases.
For each test case, the first line contains n, and the next line contains n integers.

Output:
For each test case, print the count of distinct elements on its own line.

Constraints:
1 <= t <= 100
1 <= n <= 10^5
-10^9 <= each element <= 10^9

name: Second Largest (Distinct)
statement:
For each array, print the second largest distinct value, or NONE if it does not exist.

Input:
The first line contains an integer t — the number of test cases.
For each test case, the first line contains n, and the next line contains n integers.

Output:
For each test case, print the second largest distinct value, or NONE, on its own line.

Constraints:
1 <= t <= 100
1 <= n <= 10^5
-10^9 <= each element <= 10^9

name: First Index of Key
statement:
For each test case, print the first index (0-based) at which key appears in the array, or -1 if absent.

Input:
The first line contains an integer t — the number of test cases.
For each test case: a line with n, a line with n integers, then a line with the integer key.

Output:
For each test case, print the index, or -1, on its own line.

Constraints:
1 <= t <= 100
1 <= n <= 10^5
-10^9 <= elements, key <= 10^9

name: Anagram Check
statement:
Two words are anagrams if one is a rearrangement of the other. Print YES or NO.

Input:
The first line contains an integer t — the number of test cases.
Each of the next t lines contains two words a b.

Output:
For each test case, print YES or NO on its own line.

Constraints:
1 <= t <= 1000
1 <= |a|, |b| <= 100

name: Sort Ascending
statement:
For each array, print its elements sorted in non-decreasing order, separated by single spaces.

Input:
The first line contains an integer t — the number of test cases.
For each test case, the first line contains n, and the next line contains n integers.

Output:
For each test case, print the sorted array on its own line, values separated by single spaces.

Constraints:
1 <= t <= 100
1 <= n <= 10^5
-10^9 <= each element <= 10^9

name: Prime Check
statement:
For each n, print Prime if it is a prime number, else Not Prime. Note 1 is not prime.

Input:
The first line contains an integer t — the number of test cases.
Each of the next t lines contains one integer n.

Output:
For each test case, print Prime or Not Prime on its own line.

Constraints:
1 <= t <= 1000
1 <= n <= 10^12

name: Count Primes up to N
statement:
For each n, print how many prime numbers are <= n.

Input:
The first line contains an integer t — the number of test cases.
Each of the next t lines contains one integer n.

Output:
For each test case, print the count of primes <= n on its own line.

Constraints:
1 <= t <= 50
0 <= n <= 10^6

name: Modular Exponentiation
statement:
Compute (a^b) mod m for each test case using fast exponentiation.

Input:
The first line contains an integer t — the number of test cases.
Each of the next t lines contains three integers a b m.

Output:
For each test case, print (a^b) mod m on its own line.

Constraints:
1 <= t <= 1000
0 <= a, b <= 10^9
1 <= m <= 10^9

name: Most Frequent Element
statement:
For each array, print the element that occurs most often. If several tie, print the smallest of them.

Input:
The first line contains an integer t — the number of test cases.
For each test case, the first line contains n, and the next line contains n integers.

Output:
For each test case, print the most frequent element on its own line.

Constraints:
1 <= t <= 100
1 <= n <= 10^5
-10^9 <= each element <= 10^9

name: Longest Common Subsequence
statement:
For each pair of words, print the length of their longest common subsequence (LCS).

Input:
The first line contains an integer t — the number of test cases.
Each of the next t lines contains two words a b.

Output:
For each test case, print the LCS length on its own line.

Constraints:
1 <= t <= 100
1 <= |a|, |b| <= 1000

name: Maximum Subarray Sum
statement:
For each array, print the maximum sum over all non-empty contiguous subarrays (Kadane's algorithm).

Input:
The first line contains an integer t — the number of test cases.
For each test case, the first line contains n, and the next line contains n integers.

Output:
For each test case, print the maximum subarray sum on its own line.

Constraints:
1 <= t <= 100
1 <= n <= 10^5
-10^9 <= each element <= 10^9

name: Edit Distance
statement:
For each pair of words, print the minimum number of single-character insertions, deletions, or substitutions to turn a into b (Levenshtein distance).

Input:
The first line contains an integer t — the number of test cases.
Each of the next t lines contains two words a b.

Output:
For each test case, print the edit distance on its own line.

Constraints:
1 <= t <= 100
1 <= |a|, |b| <= 1000

name: 0/1 Knapsack
statement:
For each test case, choose a subset of items with total weight at most W maximizing total value. Each item may be used at most once.

Input:
The first line contains an integer t — the number of test cases.
For each test case: a line with two integers n and W, followed by n lines each containing two integers weight and value.

Output:
For each test case, print the maximum total value on its own line.

Constraints:
1 <= t <= 50
1 <= n <= 100
1 <= W <= 1000
1 <= weight, value <= 10^6

name: Minimum Coin Change
statement:
For each test case, find the minimum number of coins to make exactly amount, using an unlimited supply of each denomination. If impossible, print -1.

Input:
The first line contains an integer t — the number of test cases.
For each test case: a line with two integers amount and k (number of denominations), followed by a line with k coin values.

Output:
For each test case, print the minimum number of coins, or -1, on its own line.

Constraints:
1 <= t <= 50
0 <= amount <= 10^4
1 <= k <= 50
1 <= coin value <= 10^4
